Name: local_trl_grpo_baseline
Title: Local GRPO Baseline on GSM8K using HuggingFace TRL
Short Hypothesis: Running GRPO with HuggingFace TRL locally on small models (0.5B–1.5B) provides a fast, reproducible baseline for reward-shaping and curriculum experiments without managed-training API dependencies.
Related Work: TRL (von Werra et al., 2020) provides a unified RL framework for transformers. GRPO (Shao et al., 2024) eliminates the need for a separate value network by normalizing rewards within groups. Local execution enables rapid iteration on hyperparameters and reward designs before scaling to managed infrastructure.
Abstract: Managed-training platforms like Tinker offer convenient GPU clusters but introduce API latency, queueing variability, and cost constraints that complicate reproducible research. We propose a local TRL-based GRPO pipeline that runs on consumer GPUs (or CPU) with small instruct models (0.5B–1.5B parameters). The pipeline uses LoRA adapters, binary exact-match rewards on GSM8K, and reports first_5_accuracy, last_10_accuracy, peak_accuracy, and training_loss across NUM_SEEDS seeds. We use this local baseline to validate reward-shaping hypotheses before committing to larger managed runs.
Experiments: Baseline run: Use the default reward_fn (binary exact match on \boxed{}) with MODEL='Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct', LORA_RANK=16, GROUP_SIZE=4, STEPS=30, LR=5e-6, NUM_SEEDS=3. Record first_5_accuracy, last_10_accuracy, peak_accuracy, training_loss, duration_seconds.
Reward-shaping ablation: Modify reward_fn to give partial credit for formatted answers, intermediate numerical overlap, or conciseness. Compare against binary baseline on the same local hardware.
Curriculum ablation: Modify curriculum() to sort GSM8K problems by difficulty (operator count) and expose easiest 30% first, then 60%, then 100%. Compare against uniform random sampling.
Group-size sweep: Run GROUP_SIZE in {2, 4, 8, 16} at fixed STEPS=30 to observe the variance–compute trade-off locally before scaling to managed infrastructure.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Small model size (0.5B) limits reasoning capacity; effects may not generalize to 8B+ models.
Local training is slower per step than managed clusters for large batch sizes, but faster for debugging and hyperparameter search.
CPU-only execution is very slow; GPU is strongly recommended even for 0.5B models.
The simplified policy-gradient loss in the template is not identical to TRL's GRPOTrainer implementation; numerical differences are expected.
GSM8K is the only task in the local template; tool-use and code tasks require additional environment setup.
